text:
multiplied by four and divided by nine. The remainder is to
be counted from Asvini, Magha, or Mula; or from Dhanishtha,
Mrigasira or Chitra. Mr. Rajagopala Iyyer wanted ns to add
one to the remainder. We do not approve this. Still we are
giving the tables as this also may form as basis for the sake of
further explanation.
This result is to be corrected further. The time of birth in
local time has to be reckoned from the local mean time of
Sunrise. Convert this interval into Vighatis. Multiply this four
by three and divide the product by seven. The remainder has
to be counted from Sunday or Friday. This mnst agree with
the day of birth.
We are presenting three sets of tables. The first agrees
with one given in Uttara Kalamrita along with the correction
necessitated by the weekday. The second table is as per Raja-

Day - Constellation Complex 19
gopala Iyyer's method. The third ignores the addition of one to
the remainder for arriving at the birth star.
